- Full Rabies vaccination was done in December 2018.
- Current issue - Oozing wound on great toe with pet having undergone vaccination too.
Given the full course of Rabies vaccination completed recently (December 2018) and considering the current situation involving an oozing wound possibly related to a pet encounter, we would typically proceed under the guidance that someone fully vaccinated does not necessarily require both Human Rabies Immune Globulin (HRig) and additional doses of vaccine according to established guidelines.
\boxed{A} Based on the evidence suggesting that individuals already vaccinated do not routinely require HRig along with booster shots and because the duration since their last dose appears recent enough, option A stating 'No vaccine required' aligns best with standard practices outlined here. It indicates no additional intervention might be needed beyond typical follow-up observations regarding the healing process and ongoing hygiene practice concerning pets.
